raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I contacted Best Buy multiple times to cancel the order and request a refund. The XXXX driver was attempting delivery to an incorrect address, and I was informed that only Best Buy could cancel the shipment. Despite clearly explaining the situation, I was told cancellation was not possible. I have retained chat transcripts and policy documentation that support my claims. 
I also requested arbitration three times, and each request was ignored. My credit card company and XXXX have declined to assist, leaving me without resolution or the product. 
Under the Georgia Fair Business Practices Act ( O.C.G.A. 10-1-390 et seq. ), consumers are protected against deceptive or unfair business practices. Refusing to cancel a misdirected shipment, ignoring arbitration requests, and failing to refund a product that was never properly delivered may constitute violations of this statute. 
I am therefore requesting a full refund of {$1400.00}. I was told by the credit card rep that they will only work with Best buy and let them decide. This makes no sense since the credit card is independent of Best Buy. I have a miss delivered package. It was forged by XXXX and I proved that to them already. They delivered to the wrong address. Even the attempted address was wrong. I contacted all three parties and each pointed the finger back to best buy who would not even listen to the issue.
